rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no corrected proof of service of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, with a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice, has been filed. (2) A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to a petition. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (3) The Wells Fargo checking account statement ending 4/25/23 (when the accounting period goes through 4/30/23) shows an amount that is less than the amount shown in the accounting. No reconciliation is shown. (4) The Wells Fargo statement ending 4/7/22 for account xxx6790 shows a balance of $123,499.10. The accounting and I&A show $125,438.10 as of 4/18/22. No reconciliation is shown. Deadline to provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner is 3/30/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and DIVIDED FROM THE REST OF THE DOCUMENT PAGE BY A PRINTED LINE. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NIKOLAOS ANASTASIOS GEORGIOU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/20/26 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice (with a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it) has been filed. (2) A courtesy copy of the petition was never received by the assigned examiner, delaying review. It appears, however, that the vehicle has not yet been sold. The estate cannot close. The administrator has not yet updated the Court (or the heir) regarding the status of the vehicle's sale, and the petition has not been amended to distribute it to her. (3) There appear to be two different amounts of cash on hand alleged in the petition. (4) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (5) Attorney costs do not indicate who the payee(s) is/were. (6) Has the creditor's claim not been paid? The petition prayer seems to indicate it has not. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/6/26 (and a revised proposed order, if applicable). If the petition is amended, notice must be given of the amendment after the amendment is e-filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEIL W. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An AMENDMENT was required to be filed to include the new request for a $12,000 reserve and to set forth the specific assets to be distributed, not just the (fluctuating) value of the assets. (2) The proof of service, filed on 11/19/25, also does not show the title of the pleading that was filed. The hearing is of the petition, not an order. The full caption of the petition and the AMENDMENT to the petition, after the AMENDMENT is e-filed, must be shown in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing. Deadline to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the AMENDMENT and proof of service of the petition and amendment is 3/20/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No revised proposed order or courtesy copy was received by the assigned examiner by the deadline of 12/3/25. The "errata" was skimmed online in hopes of being able to pre-grant the petition, but there are still (or new) issues. (2) A Court-appointed fiduciary is required to sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to his petition(s). [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (3) If there is $204,773.07 in cash on hand, if all of it is to be distributed to the two heirs, how will attorney fees and costs be paid? (4) Please read LRSF 14.35 I.6: "[T]he petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the... net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary." Deadline for providing courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner is 3/30/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A proper response to issues posted by an examiner is either a supplement that provides additional information or an amendment that corrects allegations (or a fully amended petition), not a "response" or an "errata." Issues raised by an examiner are not part of the record unless made so by the petitioner's attaching a copy of notes or tentative rulings to filed papers. A corrected proof of service should just be filed, and a proposed order should just be submitted; there is no need to narrate what the administrator will do to correct notice or provide a missing order.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZACHARY DANIEL BARANZINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed: (1) Need an amended petition or an amendment to the petition clarifying which assets and the percentage of said assets are subject to this petition #7a and #7b, respectively. CRC 7.3. Re-notice is required. CRC 7.53. The petitioner's statement (mentioned in (2) below) has no proof of service. (2) Petitioner's supporting statement filed on 12/11/25 now claims only UBER RSUs. She seeks to have 10,982 Uber RSUs (21,090 on the date of death minus 10,108 on the date of marriage) determined as passing to her without administration, alleging they are community property. However, the decedent left his community property to the Trust, not to the petitioner. The assets are subject to administration. Prob. Code sec. 13501(b). (3) It appears that Petitioner seeks the Court's confirmation of her separate property. The petition needs to provide authority for the court to confirm the surviving spouse's separate property, which would not have been subject to administration of the decedent's estate. This petition is not for splitting assets between the surviving spouse and the decedent's estate after a short marriage. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
